title: Start
body:
//主角整理了一下衣服，好像剛剛換好衣服
Thought: Ugh, my head...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服1_側站低頭)>>
Thought: This feels like...
Frances Cheng(c): Hi! Good morning!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服1_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Morning!
    Frances Cheng(c): Ha! I see you rested well!
->Rested and ready to go.
    Frances Cheng(c): Great!
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng(c): Perfect timing. I'm almost there.
Frances Cheng(c): See you at Zhang's!
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Zhang's?
    Frances Cheng(c): Zhang's Noodle, the one on the way to the subway station.
    Frances Cheng(c): Owner's a grumpy old man, but his wife is nice. Remember?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng(c): I may be a little bit late. Wait for me if you're there first.
Frances Cheng(c): Oh, don't forget about your gift!
Frances Cheng(c): Our 4th anniversary! Exchange gifts after work!
Frances Cheng(c): Bye!
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Bye.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<CloseCommunicator()>>
//沒拿禮物
<<#If($FirstDayTakeRing=false)>>
    Thought: Gotta get my gift.
    Thought: I hid it on top of the shelf by the door.
<<#EndIf>>
//有拿禮物了
<<#If($FirstDayTakeRing=true)>>
    Thought: I got the gift already yesterday. Now let's go out.
<<#EndIf>>
<<#End>>

title: 到捷運站跟芳一起等車
body:
Frances Cheng: The next shuttle will be here soon. Won't take long.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_背站轉頭)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Why are there Vision shuttles in a public subway station?
    Frances Cheng: Because Vision funded the subway system.
    <<#Simple(10)>>
    ->The government doesn't have the money?;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
        Frances Cheng: Depends on your definition of "having money".
        Frances Cheng: If you mean having that much cash in their bank account, yes. But Wanpei has been on a spur of expansion over the past decades. Too many things to spend money on.
        <<@讓芳芳解釋世界觀拉耶>>
        Frances Cheng: Plus the whole elevation plan, in preparation for the flooding...;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭返回)>>
        <<#Simple(10)>>
        ->What flooding?
        ->Elevation plan?
            Frances Cheng: Hey, Mr. Dai. Have you been reading your news at all?;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
            Thought: Why am I asking? I know about these things.
            Frances Cheng: The government had been talking about the flooding since we were in elementary school.
            Frances Cheng: Because of global warming, sea level has been on the rise. By 2053, some cities on the west side, plus Wanpei, will be sleeping under water.
            Frances Cheng: Wanpei sits in a basin. We are expected to be living in a lake by then!
            Frances Cheng: The government started the Rise 50 initiative in response. For Wanpei, the plan is called Project Plateau.
            Frances Cheng: Specifically, the plan is to scoop certain parts of Wanpei up, with suspension technology.
            <<#Simple(8)>>
            ->Whole parts of the city floating in the sky!?
                Frances Cheng: Huh? Why so surprised?
                Frances Cheng: Relax. Not really floating in the sky.
                Frances Cheng: The idea is to jack parts of the city up, slowly and steadily, with suspension technology. Then build supporting frames beneath them.
                Frances Cheng: A lot of areas are already elevated, and the number is increasing.
            ->...
            <<#EndSimple>>
            Frances Cheng: See? Our block, and the one on the other side? We're all elevated.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭返回)>>
            Frances Cheng: Also, thanks to the suspension/support, we don't feel earthquakes as much.
            Frances Cheng: A nice bonus, right?;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
            <<#Simple(8)>>
            ->Are there areas not elevated?
                Frances Cheng: Of course. For those parts, residents are evacuated.
                Frances Cheng: The evacuation process created a whole heap of new problems, though...
                Thought: She seems to have remembered something unpleasant.
            ->So, is this flooding thing a real threat?
                Frances Cheng: Some believe it's not, saying it's fabricated to legitimatize excessive spending.
                Frances Cheng: Make an excuse for large budgets, and split the money with big corporations, so on and so forth.
            ->...
            <<#EndSimple>>
            Frances Cheng: OK, enough about that!
            Frances Cheng: I'm not an elementary school teacher, you know.
            Frances Cheng: Do your own homework!;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭返回)>>
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->You mean, the subway system is Vision's property?;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
        Frances Cheng: No. The government has the ownership.
        Frances Cheng: Vision, as the provider of funding, has controlling rights. Big corporations do things like this all the time.
        Thought: Like Growell and Sunrise. They control a lot of organizations through deals like this.
        Frances Cheng: The government couldn't stretch its resources. Or else they wouldn't allow this to happen.
        Frances Cheng: Of course, there are conspiracies saying that's the plan all along.
        <<#GoTo(@讓芳芳解釋世界觀拉耶)>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>

Frances Cheng: ...
Frances Cheng: Ah, right.
Frances Cheng: I'll have to stop by the HQ tower. Can't go to the farm together with you.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Roger, ma'am.
    Frances Cheng: Be a good boy while I'm away.
->Is there anything I should know?
    Frances Cheng: Nothing, really.
    Thought: I can tell she's not lying.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng: Just some work stuff. Won't take long.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭返回)>>
Frances Cheng: We can go home together!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_背站轉頭返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 麵店跟程芳會合
body: Sophie Qiu: Hi! Sophie Qiu: Mr. Dai!;<> Sophie Qiu: Ms. Cheng is already here!;<>;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Good evening. Sophie Qiu: Yeah, good evening. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> //瞬移主角到座位上 Frances Cheng: Come on in! I thought you'd be here first, haha.;<>;<> Thought: OK. I'd like ramen with a fried egg. Frances Cheng: I already ordered for you. Ramen with a fried egg, yes? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Right. Thank you. Frances Cheng: Hee hee. <<#GoTo(@戴士傑怎麼老吃差不多的東西)>> ->How did you know? Frances Cheng: You order the same thing every single time, okay? <<@戴士傑怎麼老吃差不多的東西>> Frances Cheng: Funny. You're an excellent cook, yet you eat basically the same thing every day.;<> Thought: Because familiar things make me feel secure... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Because familiar things make me feel secure. Frances Cheng: Ah! Frances Cheng: I see! Thought: She has such cute smiles. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> ->Actually, I was about to order something else. Frances Cheng: Eh!? Frances Cheng: Sorry, I thought you were having the usual routine. <<#EndSimple>> Sophie Qiu: Ha, never seen you here so late.;<>;<> Frances Cheng: Because he's now working night shifts. Sophie Qiu: Ah, the work must be tough. Frances Cheng: Not at all! Right?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. I'm getting used to it pretty fast. Frances Cheng: The work is tough, my boyfriend is tougher, ha! ->Yeah, super tough! Frances Cheng: Is it? Haha! <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Oh, where's Mr. Zhang? Haven't seen him in a while.;<>;<> Sophie Qiu: He's on a business trip. Thought: Business trip? For running a noodle house? <<#Simple(6)>> ->Business trip? For running a noodle house? Sophie Qiu: Right, we do have business trips. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Like, finding new ingredient suppliers? Sophie Qiu: Wha- Ah, right! Something like that. Sophie Qiu: Don't let me interrupt you love birds. Enjoy!;<> Frances Cheng: Thanks!;<> //兩人吃了一下，餵個飲料？ Frances Cheng: Hey, J, about the march...;<>;<> Thought: She hasn't given up on it? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Do you still want to go? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Yeah. ->What march? Frances Cheng: The one we talked before... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: They have finalized the plan. The march is happening next month. Everything is ready. Just need more people.;<> Frances Cheng: I want to go. Frances Cheng: They are delivering a very important message, one that could potentially help many...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Could you tell me again what they're marching for? Frances Cheng: They want universal basic income.;<> Frances Cheng: A system that ensures everyone receives enough money to cover basic life expenses. Frances Cheng: We talked about this. Remember?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Meaning the government pays everyone a salary? Frances Cheng: Yes, but not exactly a salary.;<> Frances Cheng: It's a fixed number. No less, no more. <<#GoTo(@程芳說明全民基本收入一個人發多少錢是怎麼算的？)>> ->What do you mean by "basic life expenses"? <<@程芳說明全民基本收入一個人發多少錢是怎麼算的？>> Frances Cheng: The idea is to find a standard figure based on domestic consumer prices. Frances Cheng: This figure should be able to cover three meals a day and a roof over your head. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Hey, does that mean now you're interested?;<> Thought: She's beaming. Fran likes to talk about social movements. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Definitely. Would you like to talk a bit more about it? Frances Cheng: Super duper like to! Thought: I sense a torrent of a lecture coming... Frances Cheng: Ah!;<> Frances Cheng: We don't have that much time. Maybe you ask, I answer? Frances Cheng: Come on! Bombard me with questions! <<@芳解釋ubi>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Where does this salary money come from? ($FangExplainWhereMoneyComeFrom);//選過消失 <> <> Frances Cheng: First of all, the government will need to terminate most, or all, social security and welfare programs.;<> Frances Cheng: Then tax the rich people harder, especially the big corporations. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sounds like communism to me. Frances Cheng: Not really, haha.;<> Frances Cheng: More like socialism, maybe. <<@這樣不會不公平嗎？人家努力賺錢耶>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Isn't that unfair to the hardworking people? Frances Cheng: Unfair, from a personal perspective. <<@這樣會壓抑競爭啦>> Frances Cheng: It could make working hard pointless. Why try so hard when the result is the same? Frances Cheng: But I believe there should be a sweet spot between helping the disadvantaged and taxing the more capable. We'll never find out without putting the discussion on the table first. ->I think they are pretty much the same. Frances Cheng: Because they are both taking resources away from the advantaged? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Right. Frances Cheng: I see. Frances Cheng: Yeah. Opinions could differ. ->But that would kill the competition. Frances Cheng: You're right. Still, the more pressing issue is to help the disadvantaged... <<#GoTo(@這樣會壓抑競爭啦)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Anything else? <<#GoTo(@芳解釋ubi)>> ->The rich won't be happy about it. Frances Cheng: Yes. That's why we are asking the government to write it into the laws.;<> Frances Cheng: The march is important for this very reason. It may not make any immediate difference, but we must make our voices heard. <<#GoTo(@這樣不會不公平嗎？人家努力賺錢耶)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->By "universal", do you mean everyone? The rich included? ($YouMeanEveryOne);//選過消失 <> <> Frances Cheng: Yes, right.;<> Frances Cheng: It's easier to manage that way. Otherwise we must discern who needs the money and who doesn't on a case-by-case basis...;<> Frances Cheng: That means a lot of administrative spending, and it may eventually become a privilege. Frances Cheng: That's something we don't want to see.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Hold on. If everyone gets paid for doing nothing, who's gonna do all the work? Frances Cheng: I believe people will still want to work. Frances Cheng: After all, universal basic income is supposed to cover basic expenses for survival only. It can't afford a comfortable life. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I doubt it. There are tons of people who'd be happy with just basic expenses. Frances Cheng: You have a point. More research is needed, definitely. ->I see your point. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: How is it? Do you understand the idea now? <<#GoTo(@芳解釋ubi)>> ->I think I'm all good. ($TellFangYouUnderstandUbi);//問過起碼一題才出現 Frances Cheng: Wonderful!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you for explaining it all. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You... <<#Simple(8)>> ->I what? ->Something wrong with my face? Frances Cheng: Nothing... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: You're somehow different, I think. Yesterday, too.;<> Frances Cheng: Something's off about you, but I'm not quite sure...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nah, it's the same old me. Frances Cheng: Yeah. Frances Cheng: Okay...?;<> ->Probably because of the new job. I'm trying to cope with the change. Frances Cheng: Haha, you haven't officially got the job yet!;<> ->Because I'm not Jason Dai, that's why. Frances Cheng: What...?;<> Frances Cheng: Not funny at all. Dumb sense of humor. Definitely my old Jason Dai. <<#EndSimple>> ->The march that we were talking about...? Frances Cheng: Oooh, right!;<> ->... <> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: OK, back to the topic... <<#EndSimple>> ->Maybe next time. We're gonna be late for work. ->Sorry, not really. Frances Cheng: Eh? Frances Cheng: I knew you'd say that.;<> Frances Cheng: You're never interested. I shouldn't have expected any different this time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Because we're really gonna be late for work. Frances Cheng: I know. Haha! ->Yeah, shouldn't have expected. Frances Cheng: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Okay, I'm not gonna force you! <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Anyhow, I want to participate in this march. Thought: No. We talked about it before. It's too dangerous. Frances Cheng: Wait!;<> Frances Cheng: Before you say it's too dangerous! Frances Cheng: This time it's different. The march is now approved by the government. Thought: Approved? It's been years since the government approved a march of any kind.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Approved? Really? Frances Cheng: You don't trust me? Thought: Fran looks a bit offended... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No, I'm just surprised. Frances Cheng: Ha, I see.;<> ->No, I don't trust them. Frances Cheng: Hmph, I thought you knew me. Frances Cheng: Am I the naïve kind? I've verified. Relax.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Yeah, it's been a while since the government approved any legal gathering.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How did they get the approval this time? Frances Cheng: ...It's complicated. Thought: She doesn't want to go into details... <<#Simple(10)>> ->Fran? <> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: They filed the petition with a different purpose, saying it's a celebration of sorts. Thought: That only makes the problem worse! When the cops see their banners... Frances Cheng: But an approval is an approval. As long as we don't try anything violent, the police can't touch us.;<> ->Okay, I don't have to get to the bottom of everything. Frances Cheng: Ah, um...;<> Thought: She looks surprised that I didn't press the issue. Thought: I should have done it, though...;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: What do you think? With approval, the march is legal and safe.;<> Frances Cheng: Now would you agree to let me go? Thought: I still think it's dangerous. Public gatherings like this could spin out of control on short notice.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. It sounds like a great social movement. Thought: Do I really... Frances Cheng: You... Frances Cheng: You don't mean it, do you...? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Why? You really have my support. ->I mean it, seriously! Frances Cheng: You are weird today, J. Frances Cheng: But weird in a good way! ->Of course. It doesn't have much of a point, and it's dangerous. Don't go. <<#GoTo(@傑哥不同意芳芳)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@傑哥同意芳芳去遊行了程芳開心>> Frances Cheng: Yay!;<> Frances Cheng: I thought we'd have a fight over it again, with you refusing to listen. Frances Cheng: It's been like that all along... Thought: She's genuinely happy... Thought: Maybe I shouldn't have tried to stop her from the beginning... Frances Cheng: Usually I do the talking, while you just sit there sulking. Still, a one-sided fight is a fight. Haha!;<> Thought: If she's going, I should go, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wait. I'm going with you. Frances Cheng: Great! Yay!;<> Frances Cheng: I'll introduce you to my friends there. They are dying to see you in person! Frances Cheng: Ah, but you don't like hanging out with people, right?;<> Thought: Right... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Right. Frances Cheng: Then leave it to me. I won't let them get close to you. Promise!;<> Frances Cheng: I will protect you this time. Ha! ->Not at all. It's good to make new friends. Frances Cheng: Wow, Jason Dai wants to make new friends!?;<> Frances Cheng: You're like a totally different person! Frances Cheng: But I like this version of you better. Haha!;<> Thought: This version...? Frances Cheng: The old version was kind of gloomy, not caring much about others. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Haha, totally agree with you. Frances Cheng: As if we're talking about someone else! Thought: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: But don't change too much, J.;<> Frances Cheng: Jason Dai is mine and mine alone, hee hee. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Ugh, that hee hee is a bit over the top. Frances Cheng: Ha, sometimes I want to show the cutie girly side, too. ->I'll keep that in mind. Frances Cheng: Good to know! ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Hmm, I won't say no. Frances Cheng: Really? Great! Frances Cheng: I was afraid you wouldn't let me! <<#GoTo(@傑哥同意芳芳去遊行了程芳開心)>> ->I still think you shouldn't go. Frances Cheng: ...Just like every time.;<> Frances Cheng: But this is a rare opportunity. We pushed very hard to come this far... Thought: Right. Fran's been involved all along. Frances Cheng: I want to stay with everyone else to the end of it. Full closure. Frances Cheng: On the other hand, I don't want to have you worried... Frances Cheng: Trust me, J. This march is absolutely safe.;<> Frances Cheng: Even if something goes wrong, I will get to safety before it's out of control. Thought: Yet... Frances Cheng: Would you still say no? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Go ahead. Frances Cheng: R-really? <<#GoTo(@傑哥同意芳芳去遊行了程芳開心)>> ->No. Too much risk. <<#GoTo(@傑哥不同意芳芳)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->No. I won't let you. <<@傑哥不同意芳芳>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Hear me out, J.;<> Frances Cheng: This march is very, very important to me, personally. Frances Cheng: I've been doubting my job at the farm, that I may not be doing the right thing. Frances Cheng: This is different. I'm 100% sure it is the right thing to do. Frances Cheng: I don't mean universal basic income. I mean this march itself, making our voice heard.;<> Frances Cheng: This is the closest thing to a real social movement since the Moonflower Movement! Frances Cheng: I believe we are standing up for a cause. But... Frances Cheng: You don't care about causes, right? <<#Simple(8)>> ->I do care, but I can't deal with the idea of you being in danger. Frances Cheng: It's approved. The risk is negligible, really! //選過追問程芳怎麼通過許可的 <<#If($FakePurposeSoDanger=0)>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Approved for a fake purpose, practically asking for trouble. ($FakePurposeSoDanger) Frances Cheng: I mean... Frances Cheng: I can take care of myself. If anything goes wrong, I will try to calm everyone down. There will be no confrontation with the police. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Calm a mob down? You know it's impossible. Frances Cheng: I-I only need to— Frances Cheng: But— ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> ->Right. Because I care about you more than any cause. Frances Cheng: I know that, but... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ...;<> Frances Cheng: Okay... Frances Cheng: I will talk to them, see if they will excuse me...;<> Thought: Fran is sad. Still, I'm doing this for her own good... <<#Simple(10)>> ->Sorry, I really can't let anything bad happening to you. Frances Cheng: Yeah. ->Wait. OK. You should go. Thought: Huh!? Frances Cheng: Really!?;<> Frances Cheng: Then I'm, like, really going? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Really. Frances Cheng: Woah! ->But I'm going with you. Frances Cheng: Double great! I always wanted to bring you along! <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: YES!;<> Thought: ... Frances Cheng: But why this sudden change of heart? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't have the heart to see you that sad. Frances Cheng: I see... Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: You're a nice guy!;<> Frances Cheng: Hee hee. <> ->I thought it over and realized maybe it's not that risky. Frances Cheng: You see my point, right? <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@老張麵店段洛收尾)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Aaaah! No, stop!;<> Frances Cheng: It's not the time yet! We should save this for later! Frances Cheng: Whatever decision you made, it doesn't count!;<> Frances Cheng: I promise, swear, that the march is totally safe, risk-free! Frances Cheng: I'll totally convince you next time! <<#Simple(6)>> ->That would be great. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Hmph! <<#EndSimple>> <<@老張麵店段洛收尾>> Frances Cheng: Ah, it's about time. We should get going. Frances Cheng: Lemme see...;<> Frances Cheng: Right, the shuttle will arrive soon. Frances Cheng: Let's go.;<> Frances Cheng: See you later, Ms. Qiu!;<>;<> <> Sophie Qiu: Come again! Have a nice day! Frances Cheng: Definitely! <<#Simple(6)>> ->Bye. Sophie Qiu: Bye! ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 調查物件: 萬象建造紀念
body: //講述車站是萬象蓋的，提供大量萬象員工往返萬象工業園區更加快速

title: 紅盒成員出現
紅盒成員出現2
body:
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What's up?
->Let them cut the line? Why?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng: They are the Red Room staff.
Thought: Red Room? What's that...?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服2_背站轉頭)>>
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Red Room?
    Frances Cheng: Uh-huh.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng: Hmm...
Frances Cheng: Since you're a Vision employee now, you should know this...;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
Frances Cheng: Red Room is the science lab that is responsible for Vision's technological advancements.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->So?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng: Stay clear of it, and its people.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭返回)>>
Frances Cheng: Vision is obsessed with confidentiality, especially technologies.
Frances Cheng: The entire Red Room staff is kept anonymous. They even live in safeguarded campuses.
Frances Cheng: It's very rare to see them in the public, like these people here.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->They are taking confidentiality to a whole new level...
    Frances Cheng: You bet!
->But dressing up like that, doesn't it attract unwanted attention?
    Frances Cheng: Consider it a warning.
    Frances Cheng: Keep a safe distance when you see that uniform.
    Thought: They do have "keep off" written all over them, kinda...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng: I heard someone got investigated by Vision just because of a 5-minute chat with a Red Room researcher.
Frances Cheng: Aaand got fired and sued in the end, too.;<<Play(程芳,MBU_程芳_白袍_背站轉頭返回)>>
Thought: I should give these people a wide berth...
//許添興走過來
//許添興: 嗨！程小姐！
//想法: 誰？
//程芳: 許先生，晚安。
//許添興: 這妳男朋友喔？
//程芳: ………
//想法: 芳看起來有點不耐煩……
//程芳: 對啊，哈哈……
//許添興: 很好啊！人高馬大的！
//許添興: 應該賺不少吧？才配得上你這種女強人啊！
//程芳: 許先生，這是他的隱私，他不一定想談喔。
//許添興: 幹什麼？男人聊聊賺多少很正常吧？對吧？
//想法: 芳看起來有點擔心的看了我一眼……
//<<#Simple(99999)>>
//->我正在找工作。
//    許添興: 這樣喔，啊也是很辛苦啦。
//    許添興: 加油、加油！
//->不關你的事。
//->比你多就對了。
//    許添興: 哎呦，幹嘛這樣！
//    許添興: 只是搭個話聊聊天嘛，這麼沒人情味！
//<<#EndSimple>>
//許添興: 你看現在工作環境本來就比較差，機會越來越少啦。
//許添興: 現在這整間車站也就剩我跟一個掃地阿姨在顧。
//許添興: 哎，什麼都自動化，人都變得冷冰冰的了。
//許添興: 我以前跟很多人聊天喔，還有跟你們老總聊到天你知道嗎？
//許添興: 他都不請司機開車喔，說要體會公司員工平常上下班的生活。
//許添興: 你看，這樣多好！
